title: Kapileshwar Temple
tagline: Kapileshwar Temple — spiritual town of Bihar

overview.short: Kapileshwar Temple is situated in the Rahika region of Madhubani District, Bihar, India.
overview.description: Kapileshwar Temple is situated in the Rahika region of Madhubani District, Bihar, India. A Shiva Linga was placed at this temple by the sage Kapila. The site is located in the Rahika Vidhan Sabha constituency. This is one of the famous Shiva temples in Madhubani District.

topPlaces.name: Rahika
topPlaces.description: Rahika is a city in Madhubani district, Bihar, India. It is in the Darbhanga division Bisfi Vidhan Sabha constituency.

topPlaces.name: Bhoj Pandaul
topPlaces.description: Bhoj Pandaul or Bhojparaul is a village situated around 15 km from Madhubani, district headquarters of Madhubani district] , Bihar state, India. The village has an area of 5-6 square kilometers and a population of approximately 8,000.

topPlaces.name: Balat, Bihar
topPlaces.description: Balat is a village, situated around 12 km from Madhubani, state Bihar, India and around 18 km from Darbhanga. Balat is situated in Balat-Basauli Panchayat of Madhubani Sadar (Rahika) Block.

topPlaces.name: Aunsi
topPlaces.description: Aunsi Babhangama or Aunsi Gote is one of the developing small town in Madhubani district of Bihar India over one decade, It is situated both side of National Highway 105, this high way distance is 55 km which starts from Darbhanga that passes through Aunsi Gote to Jainagar that is nearby Nepal Border. Aunsi Gote is 20 km far from Darbhanga city to Jainagar.

topPlaces.name: Mithila Lalit Sangrahalaya
topPlaces.description: Mithila Lalit Sangrahalaya (Maithili: मिथिला ललित संग्रहालय) is a museum at Saurath village near the outskirts of the Madhubani city in the Mithila region of Bihar in India. It is also known as Mithila Lalit Sangrahalaya Sah Shodh Sansthan that translates to Mithila Lalit Museum cum Research Institute.

topPlaces.name: Saurath Sabha
topPlaces.description: Saurath Sabha is a historical village situated approximately 6 km northeast of Madhubani in the Madhubani district of Bihar, India. It is famous for its several centuries old tradition of annual gathering of thousands of Maithil Brahman to match couples during the Hindu months of Jyestha-Aasadh.

topPlaces.name: Mangrauni
topPlaces.description: Mangrauni is a village in Madhubani district, Bihar, India with a population of 16,533. The village is in the Rajnagar Vidhan Sabha constituency and the local language is Maithili.

topPlaces.name: Madhubani, India
topPlaces.description: Madhubani is a city Municipal Corporation and headquarter of Madhubani district. It is part of Darbhanga Division, in the Indian state of Bihar.

items.note: Rahika is a city in Madhubani district, Bihar, India. It is in the Darbhanga division Bisfi Vidhan Sabha constituency.

Bhoj Pandaul or Bhojparaul is a village situated around 15 km from Madhubani, district headquarters of Madhubani district] , Bihar state, India. The village has an area of 5-6 square kilometers and a population of approximately 8,000.
Balat is a village, situated around 12 km from Madhubani, state Bihar, India and around 18 km from Darbhanga. Balat is situated in Balat-Basauli Panchayat of Madhubani Sadar (Rahika) Block.

Aunsi Babhangama or Aunsi Gote is one of the developing small town in Madhubani district of Bihar India over one decade, It is situated both side of National Highway 105, this high way distance is 55 km which starts from Darbhanga that passes through Aunsi Gote to Jainagar that is nearby Nepal Border. Aunsi Gote is 20 km far from Darbhanga city to Jainagar.

Mithila Lalit Sangrahalaya (Maithili: मिथिला ललित संग्रहालय) is a museum at Saurath village near the outskirts of the Madhubani city in the Mithila region of Bihar in India. It is also known as Mithila Lalit Sangrahalaya Sah Shodh Sansthan that translates to Mithila Lalit Museum cum Research Institute.

Saurath Sabha is a historical village situated approximately 6 km northeast of Madhubani in the Madhubani district of Bihar, India. It is famous for its several centuries old tradition of annual gathering of thousands of Maithil Brahman to match couples during the Hindu months of Jyestha-Aasadh.
Mangrauni is a village in Madhubani district, Bihar, India with a population of 16,533. The village is in the Rajnagar Vidhan Sabha constituency and the local language is Maithili.
Madhubani is a city Municipal Corporation and headquarter of Madhubani district. It is part of Darbhanga Division, in the Indian state of Bihar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kapileshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kapileshwar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kapileshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (124 km) and the nearest railway station is Muzaffarpur Junction (66 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kapileshwar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kapileshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kapileshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kapileshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kapileshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Rahika, Bhoj Pandaul, Balat, Bihar, Aunsi, Mithila Lalit Sangrahalaya.
